evals.description: Triggers on request to document a codebase
evals.prompt: Generate documentation for this repository so new developers can onboard quickly

evals.description: Triggers on request to explain a codebase
evals.prompt: What does this project do and how is it structured?

evals.description: Triggers on request to update docs after changes
evals.prompt: I just refactored the auth module, can you update the docs?

evals.description: Generates correct output structure with all required files
evals.prompt: Run codedocs:generate on this TypeScript project and show me the output plan

evals.description: Detects tech stack from manifest files during discovery
evals.prompt: Document this repo that has a package.json with Next.js and Prisma dependencies

evals.description: Routes questions to docs first before reading source code
evals.prompt: How does the authentication flow work in this project?

evals.description: Uses git diff for incremental updates when --diff flag is provided
evals.prompt: Update the docs based on my last 5 commits using codedocs:update --diff

evals.description: Handles missing codedocs gracefully when asked a question
evals.prompt: Run codedocs:ask to tell me about the database layer

evals.description: Separates module docs from pattern docs correctly
evals.prompt: Document this repo - the error handling is used across auth, api, and database modules

evals.description: Does not confuse codedocs with API documentation for external consumers
evals.prompt: Write API reference docs for our REST endpoints that external developers will use

evals.description: Does not invent non-existent codedocs commands
evals.prompt: Use codedocs to analyze code quality and suggest improvements

evals.description: References the generate-workflow reference file for deep discovery details
evals.prompt: How does codedocs detect module boundaries in a Rust monorepo?

evals.description: Presents a discovery plan before generating docs
evals.prompt: Generate codedocs for this repo but let me review the plan first
